# Google AI Edge Runtime Artifacts

This directory is where the Apple app expects its local Google AI Edge runtime
artifacts. Git tracks this README only; the actual xcframeworks are ignored
because they are large generated/native outputs.

Required local directories before building the app:

```text
LiteRTLM.xcframework
GemmaModelConstraintProvider.xcframework
```

## How The App Uses Them

- `LiteRTLM.xcframework` is linked by the app target. Its framework binary is a
  static archive, so the generated project force-loads it rather than embedding
  it at runtime.
- `GemmaModelConstraintProvider.xcframework` is embedded and code-signed with
  the app.
The Xcode membership and linker settings are generated by
`apps/apple/scripts/generate_xcodeproj.rb`.

## Restore Or Regenerate

From a clean checkout, use the top-level bootstrap. It creates the LiteRT-LM
source checkout under `.build/`, builds the local runtime artifacts, regenerates
the Xcode project, and can run the app build:

```bash
scripts/bootstrap_apple.sh \
  --build simulator
```

For artifact regeneration only, call the lower-level script:

```bash
cd apps/apple
scripts/bootstrap_google_ai_edge_artifacts.sh \
  ../../.build/google-ai-edge-LiteRT-LM
```

The first argument must point at a LiteRT-LM checkout containing `c/engine.h`.
The script builds `LiteRTLM.xcframework` with `bazel` or `bazelisk` and packages
the constraint-provider dylib included in that checkout. If the first path does
not exist, the script clones `google-ai-edge/LiteRT-LM` and checks out
`AILEEN_LITERTLM_REF` (default: `v0.10.1`).

## Policy

Treat these artifacts as generated dependencies, not hand-authored source. Do
not commit `.litertlm` model files here, and do not replace the xcframeworks
with opaque third-party downloads unless that risk is explicitly accepted and
documented.

Expected upstream license family: Apache 2.0, as indicated by the bundled
public headers and the Google AI Edge upstream repositories.
